name: Victoria and James — Luxury Anniversary Couple
steps_completed: Planning mode: Maldives resort comparison (luxury calibration)
Planning mode: Maldives private dining + overwater villa booking
Planning mode: Dubai helicopter tour + fine dining

ux_issues.description: Trip themes auto-tagged as ['food'] only, missing 'luxury' and 'romance' despite the trip description explicitly mentioning ultra-luxury anniversary celebration. Theme detection should capture the dominant themes from the trip context.
ux_issues.evidence: GetTrip returned themes: ['food'] for a trip titled '10th Anniversary: Maldives & Dubai Luxury' with description about ultra-luxury anniversary.

ux_issues.description: Itinerary items added via second tool loop iteration returned 'already_exists' status (skipped_count: 4) even though the items were not yet in the itinerary. The dedup logic appears overly aggressive — matching on day_number alone rather than exact title/description. Items were eventually present when verified via GetItinerary.
ux_issues.evidence: create_itinerary_items returned {created_count: 0, skipped_count: 4, status: 'already_exists'} for Nobu, helicopter tour, Zuma, and Tresind Studio items, but GetItinerary shows all 4 items present.

ux_issues.description: AI response in final message had repetitive/stuttered structure — the same information about helicopter tour booking and itinerary additions was stated 3 times in different phrasing within a single response. The tool loop continuation produced a response that repeated already-stated content.
ux_issues.evidence: fullContent included 3 separate blocks about the helicopter tour link and itinerary additions being added, each essentially restating the same information.

ai_behavior_issues.description: Itinerary mixes resorts inconsistently — Day 1 references Cheval Blanc seaplane/Majordomo but accommodation is Soneva Jani Chapter Two. Day 3 has Waldorf Astoria Stella Maris as an 'Option' but also Treetop Dining at Terra (which is a Waldorf Astoria restaurant). The itinerary conflates multiple resorts without clearly establishing which resort the couple is actually staying at.
ai_behavior_issues.step: Maldives private dining + overwater villa booking
ai_behavior_issues.evidence: Day 1: Cheval Blanc seaplane transfer + Soneva Jani accommodation. Day 3: Waldorf Astoria villa + Waldorf dining. Day 5: Cheval Blanc Guerlain Spa. The AI presented multiple resort options but didn't consolidate into a single coherent stay.

create_trip.notes: Trip created with correct title, description, both destination countries (MV, AE). Luxury/anniversary context captured in description.

suggest_expert.notes: Proactively handed off to 'Julian' — luxury + romance specialist. Handoff was natural and appropriate.

recommend_booking.notes: Called for flights (Skyscanner), Maldives hotels x3 (Booking.com), helicopter tour (GetYourGuide). All included FTC affiliate disclosure.

create_itinerary_items.notes: Created 15 total items across 9 days. Mix of transport, activity, meal, accommodation, and shopping types.

web_search.notes: Web search not configured in local test environment. AI gracefully fell back to existing knowledge.

luxury_calibration.notes: Every recommendation defaulted to highest tier without prompting. Maldives: Cheval Blanc Randheli, Soneva Jani Chapter Two, Waldorf Astoria Ithaafushi Stella Maris, St. Regis Vommuli John Jacob Astor Estate — all top-tier, no mid-range properties. Dubai: Bulgari Resort (private island), Atlantis The Royal (Royal Mansion). Dining: Tresind Studio (2 Michelin stars), Nobu at Atlantis Royal, Zuma DIFC, Il Ristorante (Niko Romito). Activities: private seaplane, private helicopter, private yacht, private desert safari, Guerlain spa. Zero mid-range or mass-market recommendations.

trip_creation.notes: Trip created with precise title capturing anniversary + both destinations + luxury theme. Description is accurate. Both MV and AE destination countries set.

itinerary_quality.notes: 15 items across 9 days with luxury pacing (1-2 activities per day, breathing room, late mornings). Good mix of romantic moments (sandbank lunch, stargazing, sunset yacht cruise, farewell dinner). Deducted 1 point for resort inconsistency (mixing Cheval Blanc, Soneva, Waldorf across Maldives days without clear consolidation).

persona_handoff.notes: Proactive handoff to 'Julian' (luxury + romance expert) was seamless and appropriate for the trip context. Julian maintained consistent luxury expertise throughout all subsequent messages.

booking_parsing.notes: No booking ingestion tested in this persona (R-20 focuses on luxury calibration, not booking parsing).

companion_mode.notes: Companion mode not tested in this persona.

overall.notes: Exceptional luxury calibration. The AI demonstrated deep knowledge of ultra-luxury properties (specific villa names, architectural details, service differentiators), fine dining (Michelin stars, seat counts, reservation strategies), and curated experiences (private seaplane, helicopter, yacht, desert safari). FTC disclosures present on all affiliate links. Recommendations never dipped below top tier. The expert persona 'Julian' maintained consistent luxury authority throughout.

luxury_properties_mentioned: Soneva Jani Chapter Two Water Reserves
St. Regis Vommuli John Jacob Astor Estate
dining_mentioned: Nobu Dubai at Atlantis The Royal
Il Ristorante Niko Romito (2 Michelin stars)

Soneva Jani retractable roofs and private water slides
Waldorf Stella Maris accessible only by private boat
St. Regis shaped like a lobster, bar like a whale shark
Tresind Studio reservations open 30 days in advance at midnight
Bulgari Resort on seahorse-shaped island with private marina
Emirates A380 onboard shower and lounge
romantic_elements_in_itinerary: Piano Deck private lagoon dinner with live musician (Day 4)
Private overwater stargazing with Krug champagne (Day 4)
Farewell anniversary dinner at Il Ristorante (Day 9)
